dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic won on concentrated developer praise: in the Flux 2 Klein discussion, commenters directly credited Claude/Opus with keeping a substantial pure-C inference port organized, producing useful rewrites, and helping with domain knowledge during the build [E1].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic had the clearest positive signal of the day because the relevant HN discussion was concrete and firsthand: the project author said the work only became feasible once Opus was guided with persistent implementation notes, and other commenters described strong results from Claude-assisted rewrites and workflow hooks [E1]. That gave Anthropic the strongest directly supported upside among the tracked providers.

openai.judgementSnippet: Discussion split between a well-received ChatGPT math result, including praise for careful proof handling and an unusually solid AI contribution [E3], and a larger thread worrying that ChatGPT could move toward ads, SEO-like contamination, and the broader harms of ad-driven incentives [E2].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude/Opus was credited with enabling a substantial pure-C inference port by staying on top of implementation notes, supporting large rewrites, and helping with domain understanding during development [E1].

evidence.summary: In the Flux 2 Klein thread, antirez said the project only became possible once he used Opus with maintained implementation notes, while other commenters described Claude rewrites delivering big speedups and asked how much Claude helped with domain expertise.

The OpenAI ad-strategy discussion was driven by concern that ChatGPT would eventually carry ads even on paid tiers, invite SEO-like product placement, and inherit the socially destructive incentives of ad-based platforms.

The ChatGPT 5.2 Pro thread highlighted an Erdős problem solution, with commenters quoting Terence Tao praising the proof's avoidance of delicate errors and treating it as one of the clearer AI math contributions so far.
